title: Asphodeline tenuiflora
taxonomy: Asphodeline tenuiflora belongs to the family Asphodelaceae and is classified within the genus Asphodeline. It is a species of flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. The genus Asphodeline is often placed within the subfamily Asphodeloideae, which is a group of plants characterized by their narrow leaves and inflorescences. Asphodeline tenuiflora is often confused with other species within the Asphodelaceae family, such as Asphodeline luteistigma and Asphodeline albiflora. Further research may be necessary to determine the exact relationships between these species and the classification of Asphodeline tenuiflora.

It is known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds and skin conditions. intro: Asphodeline tenuiflora, also known as the slender asphodel, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asphodelaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including treating gastrointestinal issues, reducing inflammation, and relieving respiratory problems due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Asphodeline tenuiflora include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Asphodeline tenuiflora include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
